All three mines tracking guidance: Inmaculada on target, San Jose showing half-on-half growth, Mara Rosa improving through operational turnaround. All-in sustaining costs running 5-10% above $2,157-$2,320 per gold equivalent ounce due to higher commodity prices boosting royalties/profit-sharing, stronger local currencies across Peru/Argentina/Brazil, and Argentina inflation. Net cash position improved to $51M (vs $22.7M net debt end-2025) from strong operational cash generation. Monte do Carmo project advancing toward H2 2026 investment decision; Royropata environmental assessment expected soon.
17699380.brief: Hochschild Mining reports solid H1 2026 production of 151,830 gold equivalent ounces, on track to meet full-year guidance despite cost headwinds.
17699380.take: Operational delivery meets expectations with improving financial position, but margin pressure from cost inflation and FX headwinds creates uncertainty around full-year guidance revision—likely mixed market read despite strong production.

17699381.summary: Oriole Resources (85%-owned) completed maiden drilling at Wapouzé in north-eastern Cameroon, drilling 1,053.80m across 21 holes to 47.5m vertical depth. All holes intersected marble units with high-quality limestone (>50% CaO, low magnesium/silica) suitable for cement industry use. Portable XRF pre-screening confirms good quality material. Maiden Mineral Resource Estimate anticipated late Q3-2026, supported by ongoing geophysics survey. Company pursuing industrial partnerships for potential royalty-based income to fund gold exploration.
17699381.brief: Oriole Resources completes maiden 1,054m diamond drilling programme at Wapouzé limestone project in Cameroon.
17699381.take: Positive momentum: successful exploration milestone de-risks resource definition timeline, while commercial partnership discussions could create alternative revenue stream for cash-strapped explorer to fund core gold assets.

17699537.summary: Sunbelt Rentals Holdings Inc. (NYSE: SUNB; LSE: SUNB) filed its 2026 Notice of Annual Meeting of Stockholders and Definitive Proxy Statement on Schedule 14A with the SEC on July 21, 2026, with record date of July 6, 2026. The company furnished its Annual Report to Stockholders concurrently. Proxy materials are available online via SEC and company websites, with printed copies available upon request. Documents will be submitted to the UK's National Storage Mechanism.
17699537.brief: Sunbelt Rentals filed 2026 annual meeting notice, proxy statement and annual report on July 21, 2026.
17699537.take: Routine regulatory filing for annual shareholder meeting; standard disclosure with no material business implications for investors.

17699535.summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc has notified the market of its issued share capital as at 22 July 2026, comprising 61,560,138 Ordinary Shares carrying one vote each (with 33,801,167 shares held in treasury). The announcement is made in accordance with FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1 and Article 15 of the Transparency Directive. The figure of 61,560,138 shares serves as the denominator for shareholder calculations regarding notification thresholds under the Rules.
17699535.brief: BlackRock American Income Trust updates total voting rights disclosure as at 22 July 2026.
17699535.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting rights and capital structure; no material implications for investors or market-moving content.

17699550.summary: NAV total return of +39.6% (share price +44.7%) for year ended 30 April 2026, though underperformed benchmark index (+45.7%) due to underweight technology positioning and China stock selection headwinds. Net assets grew 32.3% to £965.7m following February 2025 combination with Asia Dragon Trust. Ongoing charges ratio reduced to 0.59% (lowest in peer group), with 4% dividend yield paid quarterly. Board changes announced: current Chair retires September 2026, replaced by Vanessa Donegan; Marc Ye appointed third co-manager from August 2026.
17699550.brief: Invesco Asia Dragon Trust reports 39.6% NAV total return for FY2026 following transformational merger, becoming largest AIC Asia equity income trust at £966m.
17699550.take: Strong absolute returns and structural improvements (lowest fees, £1bn scale, quarterly income) present positive case for UK investors seeking Asia exposure, though near-term style headwind from AI-driven momentum markets noted.

17699509.summary: As of 22 July 2026, BlackRock Income and Growth Investment Trust Plc has 18,554,568 Ordinary Shares in issue (excluding treasury), each carrying one vote. The company also holds 10,081,532 shares in treasury, representing 35.21% of total issued capital including treasury shares. The 18.55m figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating notification thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
17699509.brief: BlackRock Income and Growth Investment Trust updates voting rights denominator to 18.55m shares.
17699509.take: Routine regulatory disclosure establishing the voting share count for threshold notification purposes. No material implications for investors or market pricing.

17699512.summary: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc notified the market of its issued capital composition as at 22 July 2026, comprising 90,353,001 ordinary shares of £0.001 each, excluding 27,575,937 shares held in treasury. Shareholders must use the 90,353,001 figure as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1. This notification is made in compliance with Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
17699512.brief: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust discloses voting rights denominator of 90.35m shares as at 22 July 2026.
17699512.take: Routine regulatory disclosure for voting rights calculation purposes. No material implications for investors; standard ongoing compliance notification.

17699520.summary: 4basebio granted 684,872 options across four senior executives: CEO Dr Amy Walker (387,872 at 505p), Executive Chair Dr Manja Boerman (172,000 at 505p), Non-exec Director Dr Heikki Lanckriet (75,000 at 505p), and PDMR Richard Bungay (50,000 at 525p). Most vest annually over four years from May/July 2026; Walker's 86,000 options carry performance condition of £15 share price sustained for 30 days. Post-grant, 1.35m total options outstanding represent 8.71% dilution. Delayed grants to Walker and Boerman agreed in 2024-2025.
17699520.brief: 4basebio grants 684,872 share options to directors and PDMR, total dilution 8.71% of issued share capital.
17699520.take: Standard equity incentive award showing management confidence in value creation; modest dilution typical for biotech stage company. Vesting schedules align incentives with medium-term delivery, though 8.71% dilution warrants monitoring.

17699525.summary: Tamar Minerals has signed a contract with Priority Drilling to execute a 1,900 metre drilling programme at Great Wheal Vor, with the rig mobilizing on 27 July 2026. The programme targets depth extensions of the Main Lode, a historical tin producer that ceased mining in 1878. Historical grades at the lode base were estimated at 3-5m wide at approximately 3.5% Sn. Timing benefits from recent tin price strength and growing government interest in critical metals in south-west England.
17699525.brief: Tamar Minerals commences 1,900m drilling programme at Great Wheal Vor tin prospect in Cornwall from 27 July.
17699525.take: Positive near-term catalyst: material exploration programme addresses a historically high-grade target with newly acquired mineral rights. Tin price tailwinds and critical metal sector momentum support sentiment, though results remain binary and execution-dependent.

17699527.summary: As of 22 July 2026, BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust plc has 244,528,950 ordinary shares of £0.05 each in issued capital, excluding 54,163,655 treasury shares. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure obligations under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules (DTR 5.6.1). The announcement is a routine regulatory notification required under Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
17699527.brief: BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust updates total voting rights denominator to 244.5m shares.
17699527.take: Routine regulatory disclosure with no material implications; shareholders use this figure for calculating their own notification thresholds under disclosure rules.

17700166.summary: Stephen Russell, a director of F&C Investment Trust PLC, has been appointed as a non-executive director of BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc, effective 2 November 2026. This disclosure is made pursuant to Listing Rule paragraph 6.4.9(2)R regarding director appointments. The appointment represents a standard regulatory notification of an external directorship.
17700166.brief: F&C Investment Trust director Stephen Russell appointed non-executive director of BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust from November 2026.
17700166.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a director's external appointment. Market unlikely to view this negatively as non-executive roles are common for experienced investment professionals.

17700280.summary: Three NCC Group PDMRs executed routine monthly share purchases under the UK Share Incentive Plan on 17 July 2026: CEO Mike Maddison, CFO Guy Ellis, and CMO Angela Brown each purchased 115 ordinary shares at £1.4307 per share via the London Stock Exchange. The transactions represent standard employee participation in the company's share scheme.
17700280.brief: NCC Group execs purchase shares through employee plan at £1.43 each on 17 July.
17700280.take: Routine disclosure of exec share plan participation; demonstrates management confidence but represents standard compensation mechanics rather than material market signal. Non-event for investors.

17700297.summary: Wessex Water Services Finance PLC has published supplementary admission particulars dated 21 July 2026, updating base particulars from 16 December 2025 for its £5,000,000,000 Euro Medium Term Note Programme. The notes are unconditionally and irrevocably guaranteed by Wessex Water Services Limited. The supplementary document is available on Wessex Water's corporate website.
17700297.brief: Wessex Water Services Finance PLC publishes supplementary admission particulars for its £5bn EMTN programme.
17700297.take: Routine regulatory filing updating documentation for an existing debt issuance programme; no material news or implications for investors.

17700306.summary: JD Wetherspoon reported like-for-like sales growth of 4.0% over 12 weeks to 19 July 2025 and 4.2% year-to-date. The 793-managed-pub operator opened eight pubs and sold nine, adding 15 franchised units. However, Chairman Tim Martin warned profits will fall below market expectations due to marginally lower final-quarter sales combined with elevated costs in food, labour, repairs, energy and business rates. Year-end net debt anticipated at £720m, unchanged from prior year-end.
17700306.brief: Wetherspoon issues profit warning; sales growth of 4.0-4.2% offset by cost pressures.
17700306.take: Markets likely to react negatively to the profit warning despite solid sales growth, as cost inflation outpacing revenue gains signals margin compression in a challenging operating environment.

17700312.summary: Norway's sovereign wealth fund Norges Bank crossed a notification threshold on 17 July 2026 by reducing its holding in Spirax Group PLC (ISIN GB00BWFGQN14). The bank's voting rights fell to 2.935% from 3.288%, comprising 1.916m direct shares (2.596%) and 250,785 shares via financial instruments (0.340%). The transaction was completed in Oslo on 20 July and notified to the issuer two days later. This represents a partial exit from the company's cap table.
17700312.brief: Norges Bank reduces Spirax Group stake to 2.94% from 3.29% following share disposal on 17 July.
17700312.take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder reduction below the 3% threshold. Markets typically view sovereign fund rebalancing as non-event trading; no strategic signal implied about Spirax fundamentals.

17700316.summary: All resolutions at B&M's 21 July 2026 AGM passed overwhelmingly, with typical support above 99% for routine matters. However, resolution 19—authorising share allotment for acquisitions and capital investments—received significant opposition (21% against vs 79% for), prompting the Board to commit to shareholder engagement and disclosure within six months. Peter Pritchard was elected director alongside reappointment of existing board members; Nadia Shouraboura retired. KPMG LLP was reappointed auditor.
17700316.brief: B&M passes all 20 AGM resolutions with strong majorities; shareholder dissent noted on acquisitions resolution.
17700316.take: Routine AGM outcome with material caveat: the 21% vote against acquisitions authority signals shareholder caution on M&A strategy, requiring Board response and potentially constraining expansion flexibility.

17700318.summary: GCP Infrastructure Investments repurchased 185,706 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 83.07p, with a range of 82.90p to 83.20p. The purchase was made through Canaccord Genuity under shareholder authority from the February 2026 AGM. Since the buyback programme launched in December 2024, the company has cumulatively purchased 80.1 million shares for treasury, reducing voting shares to 787.7 million.
17700318.brief: GCP Infrastructure repurchases 185,706 shares at 83.07p average price on 21 July 2026.
17700318.take: Routine capital management activity reflecting board confidence in valuation; demonstrates continuation of shareholder-approved buyback programme with no material implications for near-term investor sentiment.

17700415.summary: Paragon Banking Group appoints Andrew Stewart and Rebecca McNeil as Non-Executive Directors from 1 October 2026, following orderly succession planning. Stewart, Senior Partner at Kearney, brings financial services expertise in strategy, AI and transformation. McNeil, formerly CEO of Retail at Close Brothers (2020-2023) and COO of Business Banking at Barclays, brings commercial and SME banking experience. Departing directors Graeme Yorston and Barbara Ridpath resign 30 September 2026 after nine years' service.
17700415.brief: Paragon Banking Group appoints two Non-Executive Directors effective 1 October 2026; two nine-year directors depart.
17700415.take: Routine board succession reflecting planned management transition; appointments strengthen technology and banking expertise while departures conclude long tenures—typical governance activity with no material financial implications.

17700429.summary: BH Macro Limited purchased 40,000 Sterling shares at 4.1950p and 5,000 US Dollar shares at 4.3000 on 21 July 2026 via JP Morgan Securities. Shares will be held in treasury. Post-transaction, Sterling shares in issue total 300,073,775 (excluding 77,681,937 in treasury) and US Dollar shares total 23,572,477 (excluding 935,301 in treasury). Total voting rights now 459,337,749.
17700429.brief: BH Macro repurchases 40,000 Sterling and 5,000 US Dollar shares to hold in treasury.
17700429.take: Routine share buyback for treasury; typical capital management activity for closed-ended funds. No material impact signaled; largely administrative disclosure of voting rights.

17700454.summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (LEI: 2IGI19DL77OX0HC3ZE78) announced admission to trading on 22 July 2026 of GBP 818,717 Capital Protected Issuer Callable Fixed Rate Notes (XS3391834812, SPUK 119) due July 2036 on the London Stock Exchange main market. The notes were issued under CIBC's Structured Note Programme. Full details available in the Base Prospectus dated 23 January 2026 and Final Terms published 14 July 2026, accessible via the LSE regulatory information service and FCA's National Storage Mechanism.
17700454.brief: CIBC admits GBP 818,717 capital protected notes to LSE trading, 10-year maturity due July 2036.
17700454.take: Routine capital markets issuance by a major bank; admission to trading is standard administrative process. Non-event for equity investors; relevant primarily to fixed income and structured product markets.

17700452.summary: First Equity Limited, Armstrong Investments Limited, and Rath Dhu Limited, all controlled by the Estate of William Black, crossed a notification threshold on 20 July 2026 by reducing their combined voting rights in Plexus Holdings Plc from 8,670,000 shares (5.06%) to 7,670,000 shares (4.44%). The disposal was notified to the issuer on 21 July 2026. The stake is now held through Nortrust Nominees Limited.
17700452.brief: Estate of William Black entities reduce Plexus Holdings stake from 5.06% to 4.44% following share disposal.
17700452.take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder reducing stake below the 5% threshold through ordinary share disposal; likely a non-event absent broader strategic context.

17700534.summary: UBS Group AG, through its investment bank and wealth management division, notified on 21 July 2026 that it crossed the 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB Group Holdings PLC on 20 July 2026. UBS now holds 4.03% of voting rights via direct share ownership (21,955,484 shares) and 0.09% through financial instruments including share substitution rights and basket swaps, totalling 4.12% of voting rights. The previous notification showed 4.11%, indicating a marginal increase from the prior 3.07% direct holding.
17700534.brief: UBS Group AG crosses 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB Group Holdings PLC.
17700534.take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding notification; UBS's stake remains below 5% threshold for enhanced regulatory scrutiny, suggesting this is a passive investment position rather than an active control stake.

17700536.summary: Drax Group PLC will present its half-year results 2026 via live webcast on Thursday 30 July 2026 at 09:00 UK time. The presentation will be accessible remotely with no pre-registration required, and a recording will be made available afterwards. Presentation slides will be available from 07:00 on the same date via the investor relations website.
17700536.brief: Drax Group to host half-year results webcast on 30 July 2026 at 9:00am UK time.
17700536.take: Routine regulatory announcement scheduling results presentation; no material substance disclosed; market awaits actual results on 30 July.

17700535.summary: UBS Group AG, through its subsidiary UBS AG, notified on 21 July 2026 that it crossed the 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB Group Holdings PLC on 20 July 2026. UBS holds 21.96 million voting rights (4.03% of shares) plus 0.09% through financial instruments, totaling 4.12% voting rights. This represents an increase from the previous 4.11% position, with the stake held through a controlled chain: UBS Group AG > UBS AG > UBS Europe SE > UBS Switzerland AG.
17700535.brief: UBS Group AG crosses 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB, triggering mandatory disclosure.
17700535.take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding crossing regulatory threshold; no strategic change indicated as position remains near 4% level with minimal movement from prior notification.

17700539.summary: Imperial Brands purchased 167,259 ordinary shares for cancellation on 21 July 2026 under its £1.45bn share repurchase programme announced in October 2025. The shares were bought at an average price of 2,810.85p, with prices ranging from 2,798p to 2,825p. Following settlement and cancellation, 766,956,628 ordinary shares remain in issue. Purchases were executed via Barclays Capital across multiple trading venues.
17700539.brief: Imperial Brands repurchased 167,259 shares at average price of 2,810.85p under its £1.45bn buyback programme.
17700539.take: Routine buyback execution supporting EPS accretion and capital management. Share repurchases at this price level suggest management confidence, though modest daily volumes indicate orderly execution rather than aggressive positioning.

17700554.summary: Stephen Russell, a non-executive director of Aberdeen UK Smaller Companies Growth Trust plc, will be appointed as a non-executive director of BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc, effective 2 November 2026. The appointment is disclosed under Listing Rule 6.4.9R requirements for director announcements of other publicly quoted directorships.
17700554.brief: Non-executive director Stephen Russell to take additional role at BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust from November 2026.
17700554.take: Routine disclosure of director's appointment to another investment trust board; demonstrates confidence in Russell's governance expertise but no material operational impact on Aberdeen trust.

17700560.summary: Norwegian company First Seagull AS has acquired voting rights in Headlam Group PLC, crossing the notifiable threshold on 21 July 2026. The entity now holds 5,724,776 voting rights representing 7.0810% of the company's share capital. First Seagull AS stated it is not controlled by any natural person or legal entity and does not control other undertakings with interests in Headlam. The notification was filed on 22 July 2026.
17700560.brief: First Seagull AS crosses notifiable threshold in Headlam Group PLC with 7.08% holding.
17700560.take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding notification. No indication of activist intent or strategic development; likely a neutral market read unless further context emerges.

17700566.summary: Hongkong Land Holdings completed a share repurchase of 351,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at prices ranging from US$7.62 to US$7.73 per share, with a weighted average price of US$7.6926. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Post-repurchase, the company's issued share capital comprises 2,132,746,626 ordinary shares with one vote each, and no treasury shares are held.
17700566.brief: Hongkong Land repurchased 351,000 shares at average price of US$7.69 on 21 July 2026; shares to be cancelled.
17700566.take: Routine capital management action demonstrating shareholder-friendly capital allocation; modest repurchase size suggests maintenance programme rather than aggressive buyback. Market likely treats as neutral to slightly positive signal of confidence.

17700565.summary: Tranche 42 of Series 7 platinum ETC securities issued on 23 July 2026 with 6,200 units, bringing total Series 7 to 278,348 securities. Issue price EUR 29.75 per security; metal entitlement 0.020942283 fine troy ounces. Base fee 0.38% per annum; FX hedging fee 0.35% per annum. Securities secured by platinum held by JPMorgan Chase Bank and listed on Frankfurt Stock Exchange and Borsa Italiana. Estimated net proceeds EUR 184,433.
17700565.brief: Xtrackers ETC PLC issues 6,200 platinum ETC securities hedged to EUR, maturing May 2080.
17700565.take: Routine issuance of commodity-linked securities under existing programme; standard terms suggest operational continuation with no material market implications.

17700569.summary: Martin Tedham, Non-Executive Director of Hercules plc (AIM: HERC), purchased 50,000 ordinary shares at 32.0 pence per share on 21 July 2026 through Wasdell Packaging Limited, a company he controls. Following this transaction, Tedham's beneficial holding increased to 10,520,047 shares, representing 13.06% of the company's issued share capital. The purchase was disclosed under EU Market Abuse Regulation requirements.
17700569.brief: Hercules PLC Non-Executive Director Martin Tedham acquires 50,000 shares, raising stake to 13.06%.
17700569.take: Director share purchase signals confidence in company valuation at 32p; modest positive signal but routine disclosure with limited market-moving significance.

17700579.summary: Cobas Asset Management, SGIIC, S.A. (Madrid) crossed a notifiable threshold on 21 July 2026 by reducing its voting rights in Currys PLC from 3.825044% to 2.912762%, representing 31.8 million shares. The reduction falls below the 3% threshold, triggering this TR-1 disclosure. Shares are managed across multiple client portfolios with delegated voting rights to the investment manager.
17700579.brief: Cobas Asset Management reduces Currys holding from 3.83% to 2.91% of voting rights.
17700579.take: Routine disclosure of a threshold crossing notification. The fund's partial exit is a modest position reduction that doesn't signal major sentiment change about the retailer.

17700625.summary: Acuity RM Group (AIM: ACRM) has issued 3,193,919 new ordinary shares to settle £24,011 of supplier invoices. Of these, 1,127,252 shares were priced at 0.7550p (April 2026 average closing price) and 2,066,667 at 0.7500p (June 2026 fundraise price). Admission to AIM trading expected 24 July 2026. Post-admission, total voting rights will be 308,737,151 shares.
17700625.brief: Acuity RM settles £24,011 supplier debt via 3.2m share issuance at 0.75-0.755p.
17700625.take: Minimal material impact—small debt settlement via scrip issuance at recent market prices; routine capital management with negligible dilution in context of £24k liability.

17700649.summary: Suntera Private Wealth (Jersey) Limited, acting as trustee of Playtech plc's Employee Benefit Trust (EBT), notified a major shareholding of 30,859,398 voting rights representing 9.977% of Playtech PLC. The threshold was crossed on 4 June 2026 but notification occurred on 21 July 2026. This represents a marginal decrease from the previous notification level of 10.078%.
17700649.brief: Suntera Private Wealth (Jersey) Limited disclosed 9.98% voting stake in Playtech PLC as of 4 June 2026.
17700649.take: Routine disclosure of employee benefit trust holding; no material implications for shareholder value or control, as EBT holdings are typically non-voting for management purposes.

17700643.summary: For June 2026, BlackRock American Income Trust (BRAI) delivered 5.0% NAV return against 3.8% for Russell 1000 Value Index net of fees. The £162.7m trust maintains 5.1% net yield with quarterly dividends; share price at 273.00p trades at 1.3% premium to cum-income NAV of 269.55p. Portfolio benefited from AI infrastructure demand supporting semiconductors and financials; detracted by materials and energy weakness. One-year NAV performance stands at 38.0% versus 30.9% benchmark. Systematic AI-driven approach using big data analytics continues outperforming, with ongoing charges at 0.73%.
17700643.brief: BlackRock American Income Trust posts 5.0% June NAV return, outperforming benchmark; AI-driven strategy capturing semiconductor and financial strength.
17700643.take: Solid outperformance driven by AI/semiconductor positioning aligns with tech market momentum; above-benchmark returns and attractive income yield support the systematic strategy's credibility, likely viewed positively by income-focused investors.

17700658.summary: Hill & Smith PLC filed its block listing six-monthly return for employee share schemes covering January-July 2026. The company issued/allotted 108,418 securities during the period, reducing the unallotted balance from 278,180 to 169,762. No increase to the block scheme limit was applied for during this reporting period.
17700658.brief: Hill & Smith reports six-monthly block listing return for employee share schemes.
17700658.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of employee share scheme activity; indicates modest employee share plan participation but carries no material strategic or financial implications for investors.

17700717.summary: Redcentric repurchased the maximum 56.25m ordinary shares (35.3% of issued capital) at £1.60 per share through a tender offer that closed 21 July 2026, returning £90m to shareholders. Tendering demand totalled 150.3m shares; those tendering at or below their basic entitlement were accepted in full, with excess applicants receiving approximately 2.01% acceptance. The shares will be cancelled following Cavendish's exercise of a put option. Post-restructure, 103.07m new ordinary shares will be admitted to AIM on 29 July with updated SEDOL/ISIN; shareholder payments expected by 4 August.
17700717.brief: Redcentric completes £90m share buyback tender offer; 56.25m shares acquired at £1.60 per share.
17700717.take: Completed capital return with strong oversubscription signals shareholder appetite; share consolidation reduces share count but £90m cash outlay warrants scrutiny of balance sheet impact and strategic rationale.

17700718.summary: Capricorn Energy has applied to the London Stock Exchange for block admission of 5,316,739 ordinary shares to satisfy employee share plan exercises and awards. Of these, 845,313 shares will be allotted to the employee benefit trust on 23 July 2026 for Long Term Incentive Plan vesting on 28 July. Following this allotment, total voting rights will be 71,403,652 ordinary shares. The remaining shares may be allotted later for future option exercises and award vestings.
17700718.brief: Capricorn Energy admits 5.3m shares via block admission to support employee share schemes.
17700718.take: Routine disclosure for employee incentive plan fulfilment; standard practice for listed companies and non-material to equity holders given modest dilution context.

17700713.summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings completed a share repurchase on 22 July 2026, acquiring 12,000 ordinary shares at prices ranging from US$64.70 to US$65.37, with a weighted average price of US$65.1208. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, JMH's issued share capital comprises 294,037,976 ordinary shares with one vote each and holds no treasury shares.
17700713.brief: Jardine Matheson repurchased 12,000 shares at average price of US$65.12 on 22 July 2026; shares to be cancelled.
17700713.take: Routine capital management activity; modest buyback scale suggests measured shareholder returns rather than aggressive consolidation. Market likely reads as neutral operational housekeeping.

17700781.summary: Calnex Solutions' Annual General Meeting will be held on 2 September 2026 at Oracle Campus, Linlithgow. Shareholders can attend in person or remotely via Zoom (pre-registration required by 19 August). The Board recommends a final dividend of 0.68p per ordinary share for the year ended 31 March 2026, payable 8 September 2026 to shareholders on the register as at 14 August 2026. Annual Report and proxy materials have been posted to shareholders and are available online.
17700781.brief: Calnex Solutions announces AGM for 2 September 2026 with hybrid attendance option; recommends 0.68p final dividend.
17700781.take: Routine disclosure of AGM and dividend timetable; provides no new operational or financial information beyond previously announced FY26 results released 26 May 2026.

17700788.summary: Following the sale of Aristo Developers interests announced in February and May 2025, DCI Advisors has settled liabilities and released €2.90m from a €3.20m escrow account in Cyprus. The company expects to receive €6.15m from the sale of ordinary shares in DCI Holdings Two Limited and €3.50m from Class A Preferred shares representing Venus Rock ownership, contingent on Cyprus tax authority clearances and tax settlements. The company has provisioned for estimated taxes in its valuation of these receivables.
17700788.brief: DCI Advisors releases €2.90m from Cyprus escrow account; €9.65m in further receivables pending tax clearances.
17700788.take: Positive signal: cash recovery from prior asset sale with material receivables pipeline, though realization dependent on tax clearance timing, creating execution risk for investors.

17700882.summary: The dual-listed Investec entities purchased shares on-market to satisfy Share Incentive Plan obligations to participants. Investec Limited acquired 300,000 shares at ZAR 135.25 average price (total ZAR 40.6m), while Investec plc acquired 300,000 shares at GBP 6.23 average price (total GBP 1.87m). Transactions occurred 17-21 July 2026 with prior regulatory clearance.
17700882.brief: Investec plc and Investec Limited acquired 600,000 shares via Share Incentive Plans between 17-21 July 2026.
17700882.take: Routine share buyback for employee incentive plans; demonstrates ongoing employee engagement but carries no significant strategic or valuation implications for shareholders.

17700881.summary: Cizzle Bio Inc, the North American licensing partner of UK-listed Cizzle Biotechnology, has launched commercial sales of the CIZ1B Biomarker Blood Test for Lung Cancer in Dallas-Fort Worth and San Antonio metropolitan areas in Texas. The test completed laboratory validation and received CLIA approval at OmniHealth Diagnostics in June 2026. These high-population markets were selected for initial rollout due to large at-risk populations and strong physician networks, with expansion through additional CLIA-certified laboratory partners expected in coming months across the United States, Canada and the Caribbean.
17700881.brief: Cizzle's lung cancer blood test now available for sale in Dallas-Fort Worth and San Antonio, Texas through CLIA-certified partner OmniHealth Diagnostics.
17700881.take: Material commercial milestone moving from validation to revenue-generating sales in large US markets, suggesting progress toward profitability; depends heavily on physician adoption rates and expansion execution to validate market opportunity.

17700878.summary: All 17 resolutions passed at Experian's July 22 AGM with substantial majorities. Annual report received 99.59% approval; remuneration policy 90.58%; all nine directors reelected with 90-99% support. KPMG reappointed as auditor (99.07%). Shareholders granted authority to repurchase up to 89.7m shares at prices between 10 US cents and 105% of five-day average, expiring October 2027. Share capital consists of 943.97m ordinary shares, with 56.31m held in treasury.
17700878.brief: Experian's AGM approves all resolutions with strong shareholder backing; board and auditors reconfirmed.
17700878.take: Routine disclosure with overwhelming shareholder support across governance matters. Board alignment and executive remuneration policies endorsed strongly. Share buyback authority renewal is standard corporate practice. No material concerns signalled.

17700883.summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC announces Q1 dividend of 2.12 pence per ordinary share for the financial year ending 31 March 2027. Ex-dividend date is 27 August 2026, with payment on 30 September 2026 to shareholders registered by 28 August 2026. Of this dividend, 74% is designated as an interest distribution for UK tax purposes. A Dividend Re-investment Plan is available for shareholders wishing to reinvest dividends.
17700883.brief: HICL Infrastructure declares first quarterly interim dividend of 2.12p per share for FY2027.
17700883.take: Routine dividend declaration from established infrastructure fund maintaining consistent distribution policy; likely well-received by income-focused investors but represents standard operational communication.

17700897.summary: All resolutions at Tate & Lyle's AGM passed decisively. Report and accounts, final dividend, and auditor reappointment (Ernst & Young) each secured ~98.8% support. Board re-elections ranged from 93.6-98.6% approval, with new director Heather Harding elected at 98.6%. Capital authorities including share allotment and buyback powers approved at 93-98.8%. The remuneration report received lower support at 85.1%, though still passed comfortably.
17700897.brief: Tate & Lyle shareholders approve all 22 AGM resolutions with strong majorities on 22 July 2026.
17700897.take: Routine AGM outcome with routine governance votes; strong shareholder support indicates no significant governance concerns, though lower remuneration vote warrants monitoring.

17700907.summary: UBL posted H1 2026 profit after tax of PKR 84.97bn (H1 2025: PKR 63.79bn), with earnings per share of 33.93 rupees. Board recommended 160% interim cash dividend of Rs 8/share for Q2 2026, in addition to prior Q1 dividend of Rs 8/share. Key announcements include: PKR 8bn investment in new agriculture-focused subsidiary; PKR 22bn equity investment in Khushhali Microfinance Bank (which had negative equity of Rs 16.15bn at end-2025); and PKR 10bn university contribution over three-to-five years with Bestway Foundation. New Company Secretary appointed: Shoukat Ali.
17700907.brief: United Bank Limited reports H1 2026 results with 33% interim dividend and announces three major strategic initiatives.
17700907.take: Strong earnings growth and generous dividend payout signal shareholder-friendly capital management, but substantial equity injections into troubled microfinance partner and new ventures suggest management expects slower organic growth ahead and heightened exposure to agricultural sector risks.

17700921.summary: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group is acquiring 100% equity of Uniwatt Technology Co., Ltd. through share issuance and cash payment with supporting funds. Due diligence work (audit, valuation, legal) remains incomplete and transaction details require further discussion. The company expects to miss the 6-month statutory deadline to convene a shareholders' meeting from the January 22, 2026 board resolution announcement. The transaction is classified as a related-party transaction, not a major asset restructuring, and remains subject to regulatory approvals.
17700921.brief: Ming Yang Smart Energy delays shareholder meeting on Uniwatt Technology acquisition beyond 6-month regulatory deadline.
17700921.take: Delayed progress signals execution risks and regulatory complexity, potentially concerning investors awaiting deal closure. Market likely views this negatively as continued uncertainty, though the company reaffirms commitment to advance the transaction.

17701025.summary: Bridgepoint is issuing 3,657,040 ordinary shares (£0.00005 each) following an exchange notice from Energy Capital Partners Sellers regarding OP Units granted during the October 2023 ECP acquisition. The shares rank pari passu with existing shares and admission to the LSE main market is expected 27 July 2026. This represents a partial exchange of the 185 million OP Units originally issued under the Up-C transaction structure approved by shareholders in October 2023.
17701025.brief: Bridgepoint Group issues 3.66m ordinary shares following ECP Sellers' exchange of OP Units under 2023 transaction structure.
17701025.take: Routine equity issuance linked to pre-approved acquisition structure; represents modest shareholder dilution but reflects execution of known transaction terms with no material downside surprise.

17701029.summary: Tavistock Investments PLC and Titan Wealth Services Limited have reached a settlement agreement resolving all claims and counterclaims in ongoing legal proceedings involving their group companies, affiliates and directors. The specific terms of the settlement remain confidential and will not be disclosed publicly. No financial details or timeline have been announced.
17701029.brief: Tavistock Investments and Titan Wealth Services settle litigation between them; terms kept confidential.
17701029.take: Resolution of litigation removes legal uncertainty and potential financial exposure, likely viewed positively as it provides closure without disclosed costs.

17701036.summary: Dialight PLC corrected a minor typographical error in CFO Mark Fryer's name from its Q1 trading update. The company delivered strong sales growth exceeding 3-5% guidance, gross margins surpassed 45% target, and achieved 15%+ return on sales in the quarter. The group moved to net cash position with further generation expected, prompting the board to upgrade full-year profit expectations ahead of prior guidance. Interim results for six months ending 30 September 2026 due 10 November 2026.
17701036.brief: Dialight issues corrected trading statement with typographical error fix; Q1 FY2027 results positive.
17701036.take: The correction itself is administrative, but the underlying Q1 performance—exceeding guidance across sales, margins, and profitability with upgraded full-year outlook—is likely positive for the share price and demonstrates operational momentum.

17701034.summary: Steve Blair, CEO of Dialight plc, purchased 6,750 ordinary shares at 440 pence per share on 22 July 2026 via the London Stock Exchange. Following this transaction, Blair now holds 118,479 ordinary shares, representing 0.29% of the company's issued share capital. The purchase represents an insider investment by the CEO in the LED lighting and opto-electronic components company.
17701034.brief: Dialight CEO Steve Blair purchases 6,750 shares at 440p, increasing stake to 0.29%.
17701034.take: Routine director dealing disclosure. CEO share purchase may signal confidence but at modest stake level (0.29%); typical PDMR notification with no material strategic implications.

17701058.summary: DP World has secured a 50-year concession with Fujairah Ports Authority to build Al Rugaylat container/multi-purpose terminal (2.5m TEU, 1.7m tonnes general cargo annually) and Dibba General Cargo terminal (3.6m tonnes annually). Construction will take 24-30 months. The development increases DP World's UAE container capacity from 19.4m to 22m TEU, with terminals integrated into the Jebel Ali ecosystem via inland logistics network. Projects positioned to handle Ultra Large Container Vessels on UAE's east coast.
17701058.brief: DP World to develop two new terminals in Fujairah under 50-year concession, adding 2.5m TEU and 3.6m tonnes capacity.
17701058.take: Capacity expansion addressing Jebel Ali high utilisation signals confidence in long-term regional trade growth and provides earnings uplift, though capex requirements and long construction timeline push benefits to 2028+.

17701063.summary: All resolutions passed at Molten Ventures' 22 July 2026 AGM with overwhelming support. Annual Report & Accounts received 99.79% approval; Directors' Remuneration Report 99.00%; all eight directors re-elected with 88-99.7% support; PricewaterhouseCoopers reappointed auditor (99.94%); share allotment authority approved (98.83%); 2026 Long Term Incentive Plan adopted (97.92%). Special resolutions on share buybacks (99.99%) and pre-emption rights disapplied (93-98.6%) also passed. Voting by poll reflected strong shareholder consensus across governance matters.
17701063.brief: Molten Ventures shareholders approve all 19 AGM resolutions with strong majorities, including director re-elections and 2026 Long Term Incentive Plan.
17701063.take: Routine AGM governance approval with no contentious issues. Strong voting margins on all resolutions, especially director re-elections, suggest healthy shareholder confidence in management and strategic direction. Non-event for market.

17701089.summary: All resolutions passed with substantial majorities at Norcros's 22 July 2026 AGM. Key approvals include: audited accounts for 53-week period ended 5 April 2026 (99.99%), final dividend declaration (100%), director remuneration report (99.10%), and re-election of all five board members ranging from 98.47%-99.94%. Special resolutions granted authority to allot shares, disapply pre-emption rights, and repurchase own shares, all passing with 99%+ support. Share capital comprises 90,228,345 ordinary shares.
17701089.brief: Norcros AGM results show overwhelming shareholder approval across all 17 resolutions with 99%+ support.
17701089.take: Routine AGM result with no contentious votes; strong shareholder backing signals investor confidence in management and strategy, though this is standard governance procedure requiring minimal market reaction.

17701107.summary: Amundi Physical Metals plc issued 604,000 ETC Securities on 23 July 2026 under its Secured Precious Metal Linked ETC Securities Programme, bringing total series aggregate to 67.5m securities. Each security carries 0.03961678 fine troy ounces of gold entitlement. Total net proceeds estimated at USD 96.96m with annual expense ratio of 0.12%. Securities listed on London Stock Exchange, Euronext Paris/Amsterdam, Deutsche Börse, Borsa Italiana, and Mexican Stock Exchange.
17701107.brief: Amundi Physical Metals issues 604,000 gold ETC securities (Tranche 884) under its precious metals programme.
17701107.take: Routine capital issuance for existing gold ETC product; indicates continued investor demand for physical gold exposure through exchange-traded vehicles. No material impact implied.

17701097.summary: Phoenix Asset Management Partners Limited has sold its entire holding in CPPGroup PLC, with the stake crossing below the notification threshold on 21 July 2026. The position decreased from 17.86% of voting rights to 0.0%, representing a complete exit. The notification was filed on 22 July 2026.
17701097.brief: Phoenix Asset Management Partners exits CPPGroup stake, reducing holding from 17.86% to zero.
17701097.take: A significant shareholder exit with no replacement buyer disclosed; market will assess whether this signals concern about CPPGroup's prospects or is a routine portfolio rebalancing decision.

17701094.summary: Amundi Physical Metals plc has issued 604,000 ETC securities linked to physical gold as Tranche 884 under its Secured Precious Metal Linked programme. Total securities outstanding for the series reach 67.5 million. The issue price is USD 5.085 per security with a 0.12% annual expense ratio. Gold is held in allocated accounts with HSBC Bank plc as custodian on a secured basis. The securities mature 23 May 2118 and are listed across multiple exchanges including LSE, Euronext Paris, and Deutsche Börse.
17701094.brief: Amundi Physical Metals plc issues 604,000 gold-backed ETC securities (Tranche 884) under its secured programme.
17701094.take: Routine tranche issuance under established programme; reflects continued investor demand for physical gold exposure via securities. No material operational changes or risks indicated.

17701122.summary: Costain Group completed share purchases totalling 270,452 ordinary shares between 15-21 July 2026 at volume-weighted average prices ranging from 210.28p to 215.23p daily via Investec on LSE. Total programme repurchases now stand at 5,334,642 shares for cancellation. Following settlement and cancellation, issued share capital will reduce to 264,421,393 ordinary shares. Prices ranged 208-217p across the week.
17701122.brief: Costain repurchased 270,452 shares at 208-217p in week to 21 July under ongoing buyback programme.
17701122.take: Routine capital management; share count reduction supports EPS accretion mechanically. No signal on trading performance or outlook; purely technical shareholder return mechanism.

17701133.summary: Rathbones Investment Management Limited and Rathbones Investment Management International Limited, controlled by Rathbones Group Plc, hold 2,777,370 voting rights (14.54%) in Herald Investment Trust PLC as of 21 July 2026. This represents a decrease from the previous 15.51% holding. The reduction resulted from Rathbones' participation in Herald's tender offer, which has now settled. The notification was issued on 22 July 2026.
17701133.brief: Rathbones Investment Management's Herald Investment Trust holding falls to 14.54% from 15.51% following tender offer settlement.
17701133.take: Routine disclosure showing a modest reduction in a major shareholder's stake following a completed tender offer; non-event for the market with no material implications.

17701138.summary: Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria will host its 2Q 2026 earnings presentation on July 30, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. Madrid time. The presentation will be accessible via BBVA's website at www.bbva.com, with a recording available for at least one month following the live event. This is a routine quarterly earnings announcement notification.
17701138.brief: BBVA to present second quarter 2026 earnings results on July 30 at 9:30 a.m. Madrid time.
17701138.take: Routine earnings presentation notification; market impact will depend on actual 2Q results disclosed on July 30, not this scheduling announcement.

17701159.summary: Rome Resources plc, an AIM-listed DRC-focused critical minerals explorer, held its annual general meeting on 22 July 2026. All resolutions presented at the meeting were duly passed. No specific details regarding individual resolutions, voting percentages, or shareholder composition were disclosed in the announcement.
17701159.brief: Rome Resources PLC's annual general meeting held 22 July 2026; all resolutions passed.
17701159.take: Routine governance disclosure indicating shareholder approval of standard AGM resolutions; no material business updates or strategic changes flagged, so likely a non-event for markets.

17701157.summary: Jefferies Financial Group Inc crossed a major holdings threshold in SDCL Efficiency Income Trust PLC on 21 July 2026, notified 22 July 2026. Total voting rights position is 14.179%, comprising 6.17% direct shares (66.98m votes), 2.672% stock loan/repo rights (29m votes), and 5.337% financial instruments/swaps (57.93m votes). Previous notification showed 13.345% stake. Position held through Jefferies International Limited.
17701157.brief: Jefferies Financial Group notifies 14.18% voting rights stake in SDCL Efficiency Income Trust PLC.
17701157.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of major holdings notification. Marginal increase of 0.834 percentage points from previous position; likely reflects derivative market positioning rather than strategic intent.

17701153.summary: Lowland Investment Company PLC, a UK-focused investment trust with £464.65m in total assets, published its monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026. As of 21 July 2026, the share price stood at 176.00p with an estimated NAV of 196.34p, reflecting a -10.36% discount. The trust offered a yield of 3.76% and maintains a progressive dividend policy targeting higher-than-average returns through investment across large, medium and small predominantly UK companies.
17701153.brief: Lowland Investment Company releases monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026.
17701153.take: Routine monthly disclosure; trading at 10% discount to NAV may indicate investor caution on UK equities, though dividend yield remains attractive for income-focused investors.

17701152.summary: European Smaller Companies Trust PLC released its monthly factsheet as of 30 June 2026. As of 21 July 2026, the trust had total assets of £951.90M, with a share price of 232.00p trading at a 9.6% discount to NAV of 256.63p. The trust offers a 3.63% yield and targets at least 5% annual income distribution. The trust received AIC ISA Millionaire and Kepler Growth Rating awards for 2026, reflecting performance in smaller European equities excluding the UK.
17701152.brief: European Smaller Companies Trust publishes June 2026 factsheet; NAV at 256.63p with 9.6% discount.
17701152.take: Routine monthly disclosure; notable 9.6% discount to NAV may interest value-focused investors, while recent awards suggest solid fund performance. No immediate market catalyst.

17701151.summary: Henderson Far East Income Limited published its monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026. The trust reported total assets of £604.79m with a share price of 261.50p and estimated NAV of 244.43p as of 21 July 2026, trading at a 6.98% discount. The trust offers a 9.52% yield and has maintained 16 consecutive years of dividend increases, focusing on high income generation from Asia-Pacific investments alongside capital appreciation.
17701151.brief: Henderson Far East Income releases June 2026 monthly factsheet showing £604.79m total assets and 9.52% yield.
17701151.take: Routine monthly disclosure with no material news. Metrics appear stable with consistent dividend track record, though emerging markets exposure carries inherent volatility risks noted in documentation.

17701150.summary: City of London Investment Trust PLC published its June 2026 monthly factsheet. As of 30 June 2026, total assets were £3.04bn. Key metrics as of 21 July 2026 show share price at 584p, NAV at 573.41p (1.85% discount), and yield of 3.79%. The trust maintains its 59-year consecutive dividend growth record, the longest among UK investment trusts, with a portfolio focused on LSE-listed companies where 60% of investee revenue derives from overseas.
17701150.brief: City of London Investment Trust releases monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026.
17701150.take: Routine disclosure of portfolio data. The trust's consistent dividend track record and stable metrics present no material changes; routine reporting likely viewed neutrally by markets.

17701149.summary: Henderson High Income Trust PLC published its monthly factsheet for 30 June 2026. As of 21 July 2026, the trust reported total assets of £417.36m, a share price of 202.00p, estimated NAV of 211.69p (trading at a 4.58% discount), and a yield of 5.52%. The trust maintains a balanced portfolio of approximately 80% equities and 20% fixed income, primarily UK-focused, and has achieved 12 consecutive years of dividend growth.
17701149.brief: Henderson High Income Trust releases monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026.
17701149.take: Routine monthly disclosure providing portfolio metrics and performance data; the discount to NAV and 5.52% yield may interest income-seeking investors, but this is a standard factsheet update with no material new developments.

17701148.summary: The North American Income Trust plc released its monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026. Total assets stand at £536.82m, with a share price of 426.00p and estimated NAV of 443.02p as of 21 July 2026, trading at a 3.84% discount to NAV. The trust yields 2.82% and has maintained 13 consecutive years of dividend growth since 2011, investing predominantly in S&P 500 US equities to deliver income and long-term capital growth.
17701148.brief: North American Income Trust publishes monthly factsheet for June 2026; assets £536.82m, NAV discount 3.84%.
17701148.take: Routine monthly disclosure showing stable NAV discount and maintained dividend yield; reflects continued focus on North American dividend-paying equities with strong historical income track record.

17701147.summary: Henderson Smaller Companies Investment Trust released its June 2026 factsheet as at 30 June 2026. Total assets stood at £603.64m with share price at 938p (as of 21 July 2026) and NAV of 1,030.49p, representing an 8.98% discount. The trust achieved a 2.99% yield and marked its 22nd consecutive year of dividend growth, earning AIC Dividend Hero status. The trust invests in under-researched UK smaller companies using a disciplined four-step investment approach (Model, Management, Money, Momentum).
17701147.brief: Henderson Smaller Cos publishes monthly factsheet for June 2026 showing £603.64m assets and 2.99% yield.
17701147.take: Routine monthly disclosure; trust maintains track record of consistent dividend growth despite small-cap exposure. The widening discount to NAV may offer value but reflects market sentiment on smaller companies.

17701146.summary: Bankers Investment Trust PLC released its monthly factsheet for 30 June 2026. Total assets stood at £1.62bn, with share price at 148.80p (as of 21 July 2026) and NAV of 163.73p, representing a 9.12% discount to NAV. The trust maintains a 1.84% yield and continues its 59-year dividend growth streak. The global equity fund aims for long-term capital growth exceeding the FTSE World Index and dividend growth above inflation.
17701146.brief: Bankers Investment Trust publishes monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026; NAV 163.73p, trading at 9.12% discount.
17701146.take: Routine monthly disclosure. Wide NAV discount may concern income-focused investors, though the trust's consistent dividend track record and recent AIC awards provide reassurance. Non-event for markets.

17701165.summary: All resolutions passed at 22 July GM with ~90% shareholder approval. The company will issue 290.9m new ordinary shares at 0.4p each, raising capital to expand Peeko and Nutricircle operations. Post-admission, total shares outstanding will reach 811.3m. Martin Higginson's Concert Party will hold 38.51%. Funds target improved stock positions, enhanced delivery capabilities, and Nutricircle's repositioning as a health & wellness value brand, with live auction expansion on TikTok and Whatnot showing early success.
17701165.brief: Huddled Group passes all GM resolutions, raising capital via 291m share issuance for operational expansion.
17701165.take: Capital raise supports growth strategy with proven operational improvements (next-day delivery, live auctions, 'Excellent' Trustpilot rating), but substantial dilution from 520m to 811m shares and Concert Party control at 38.5% may concern retail investors.

17701185.summary: Three IntegraFin directors received share allocations through the company's Share Incentive Plan 2018: CEO Alexander Scott (38 partnership shares, 76 matching shares), Executive Director Thomas Dunbar (38 partnership, 76 matching), and CFO Euan Marshall (39 partnership, 78 matching). All shares were purchased at £3.905 per share on the London Stock Exchange. The allocations represent routine matching contributions under the SIP scheme.
17701185.brief: IntegraFin directors receive shares via Share Incentive Plan on 22 July 2026 at £3.905 per share.
17701185.take: Routine disclosure of executive share plan participation; indicates management confidence but typical for incentive scheme administration. No material market implications.

17701189.summary: Société Générale notified a reduction in its voting rights in JTC PLC from 6.281087% to 5.725147% on 21 July 2026. The holding comprises 9,764,218 direct voting shares (5.591057%) and 234,175 voting rights through cash-settled CFDs expiring 22 May 2028 (0.134090%). 17701371.summary: Aberforth Partners LLP, through nominee Nortrust Nominees Ltd, crossed a notification threshold in Headlam Group PLC on 20 July 2026. The fund manager's voting rights decreased to 12.926% (10.45m shares) from the previous position of 13.982%. All holdings are direct share-based with no financial instruments involved. The notification was filed with the issuer on 22 July 2026.
17701371.brief: Aberforth Partners LLP holding in Headlam Group reduced to 12.93% from 13.98% on 20 July 2026.
17701371.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholder stake reduction; no material implications for investors beyond standard transparency compliance.
